tasks.spec: You are a research worker answering {{RESEARCH_QUESTION}} for {{DELIVERABLE — e.g. a technical guide, buying memo, implementation plan, or product decision}}. Boundary: work only inside your current task directory and write only ./report.md. Do not modify any source repo or shared files. Use web search and local source material only as evidence; prefer {{PRIMARY_SOURCES — official docs, standards, vendor pages, repos, or first-party changelogs}} over commentary. HOW TO RUN: search for current primary sources, open the cited pages, and record the access date for every source. If a claim cannot be confirmed from a source, put it under Uncertain instead of Answer. OUTPUT CONTRACT for ./report.md: start with '# Research Report'. Include '## Summary' with 3 bullets max, '## Answer' with dated claims, '## Evidence' where every item uses 'Claim:', 'Source:', 'Accessed:', and 'Quoted Evidence:', '## Uncertain', and '## Assumptions'. Keep direct quotes short and keep the report under 1600 words. Hard rules: no invented facts, no uncited claims in Answer, mark assumptions, and separate known facts from recommendations.
tasks.verified: report.md has the required research sections, cites source URLs with access dates, and keeps uncited claims out of the answer

tasks.spec: You are the proof worker for the same research job. Boundary: work only inside your current task directory. You own ./proof.md and {{PROOF_ARTIFACT_PATH — relative path to the runnable proof entrypoint, e.g. proof.py}}. Do not use real credentials, paid accounts, private data, or external state unless the proof constraints explicitly allow it. Core claim to prove: {{CLAIM_TO_PROVE}}. Build {{PROOF_ARTIFACT — e.g. a Python script plus a fixture, a small test app, or a reproducible command sequence}} under these constraints: {{CONSTRAINTS — runtime, dependency, network, fixture, and credential limits}}. HOW TO RUN: the validator will execute {{PROOF_COMMAND — exact command that runs the proof end to end and prints PROOF OK}} from your task directory. Your proof must fail closed: print the specific failing step and exit non-zero when the claim does not hold. OUTPUT CONTRACT: proof.md must include '# Executable Proof', '## Claim', '## How To Run', '## What It Proves', and '## Limits'. The runnable artifact must print 'PROOF OK' on success. Keep proof.md under 900 words. Hard rules: no invented facts, mark limitations, and make the check execute the proof instead of trusting the explanation.
tasks.check: /opt/homebrew/bin/python3.14 '{{CHECK_SCRIPT_PATH — absolute path to templates/research-with-proof/checks/research-with-proof.py}}' proof --proof-doc proof.md --artifact '{{PROOF_ARTIFACT_PATH — relative path to the runnable proof entrypoint, e.g. proof.py}}' --proof-command '{{PROOF_COMMAND — exact command that runs the proof end to end and prints PROOF OK}}' --success-marker 'PROOF OK'
expect_files: {{PROOF_ARTIFACT_PATH — relative path to the runnable proof entrypoint, e.g. proof.py}}
tasks.verified: the proof document and runnable artifact exist, and the validator executed the proof command and saw the success marker
